message:
docs: Update README for new onboard subcommand syntax

Update all onboard command examples from flag syntax (--status, --sample)
to subcommand syntax (status, sample) throughout the README.

Closes: cass_memory_system-yee2

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"cass_memory_system-qwm2","title":"Implement onboard subcommands in cm.ts and onboard.ts","description":"## Context

This task implements the subcommand structure designed in cass_memory_system-3i3u.

## Implementation Checklist

### 1. Update cm.ts

Replace the current onboard command definition:

```typescript
// Current (flags)
program.command("onboard")
  .description("Agent-native guided onboarding")
  .option("--status", ...)
  .option("--sample", ...)
  // etc.
```

With subcommand pattern (like playbook):

```typescript
// New (subcommands)
const onboard = program.command("onboard")
  .description("Agent-native guided onboarding (no API costs)");

onboard.command("status")
  .description("Check onboarding status and progress")
  .option("--json", "Output JSON")
  .action(async (opts) => await onboardStatus(opts));

onboard.command("gaps")
  .description("Show playbook category gap analysis")
  .option("--json", "Output JSON")
  .action(async (opts) => await onboardGaps(opts));

onboard.command("sample")
  .description("Sample diverse sessions for analysis")
  .option("--limit <n>", "Number of sessions", toInt)
  .option("--fill-gaps", "Prioritize underrepresented categories")
  .option("--include-processed", "Include already-processed sessions")
  .option("--workspace <path>", "Filter by workspace")
  .option("--agent <name>", "Filter by agent")
  .option("--days <n>", "Filter to last N days", toInt)
  .option("--json", "Output JSON")
  .action(async (opts) => await onboardSample(opts));

onboard.command("read")
  .description("Read/export a session for analysis")
  .argument("<path>", "Session path")
  .option("--template", "Rich contextual output for extraction")
  .option("--json", "Output JSON")
  .action(async (path, opts) => await onboardRead(path, opts));

// ... etc for prompt, guided, mark-done, reset
```

### 2. Update onboard.ts

Refactor from single mega-function to individual handlers:

```typescript
// Export individual handlers
export async function onboardStatus(options: { json?: boolean }): Promise<void>
export async function onboardGaps(options: { json?: boolean }): Promise<void>
export async function onboardSample(options: SampleOptions): Promise<void>
export async function onboardRead(sessionPath: string, options: ReadOptions): Promise<void>
export async function onboardPrompt(options: { json?: boolean }): Promise<void>
export async function onboardGuided(options: { json?: boolean }): Promise<void>
export async function onboardMarkDone(sessionPath: string, options: { json?: boolean }): Promise<void>
export async function onboardReset(options: { yes?: boolean; json?: boolean }): Promise<void>

// Keep internal helper functions private
async function getOnboardStatus(config, playbook) { ... }
async function sampleDiverseSessions(options) { ... }
// etc.
```

### 3. Add Deprecation Layer

Keep old flags working but with warnings:

```typescript
// In cm.ts, add a catch-all handler for legacy flags
program.command("onboard")
  .option("--status", "DEPRECATED: Use 'cm onboard status'")
  // ... other deprecated flags ...
  .action(async (opts) => {
    // Deprecation detection and forwarding
    if (opts.status) {
      console.error(chalk.yellow("⚠️  '--status' is deprecated. Use 'cm onboard status' instead."));
      return onboardStatus({ json: opts.json });
    }
    // ... handle other deprecated flags ...
    
    // If no flags, show help
    program.commands.find(c => c.name() === 'onboard')?.help();
  });
```

## Files Modified

- `src/cm.ts` - Subcommand definitions
- `src/commands/onboard.ts` - Refactored handlers

## Testing

- Test each subcommand individually
- Test deprecated flag syntax still works
- Test JSON output is unchanged
- Test error cases

## Depends On

cass_memory_system-3i3u (Design task)

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-r1hq","title":"[UX][output] Add shared terminal UI helpers (sections, tables, wrapping)","description":"Problem
- Each command currently hand-rolls formatting (rulers, headings, emojis, spacing).
- This causes inconsistent look-and-feel and makes it expensive to polish.

What to do
- Create a small shared output helper layer (no heavy UI framework) that provides:
  - Terminal width detection and safe wrapping
  - Standard section headers and separators
  - Key/value tables and aligned lists
  - Hint/warn/error callouts (with optional icons)
  - “showing N of M” truncation helper

Scope
- Used by `context`, `doctor`, `similar`, `stats`, `usage` first.","design":"Implementation sketch
- Add functions like `section(title)`, `kv(rows)`, `list(items, {limit})`, `wrap(text, width)`.
- Ensure helpers work correctly in non-TTY contexts (CI).
- Centralize icon selection behind a flag (see separate emoji/color task).","acceptance_criteria":"- A reusable helper exists and is used by at least one command.
- Output is width-aware and avoids hard-coded rulers.
- No new dependencies beyond what’s necessary (prefer internal helpers + chalk).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:16:31.464788-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T18:09:31.972515-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T18:09:31.972515-05:00","labels":["cli","output","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-r1hq","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-3tds","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-13T05:16:36.901199-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y9k","title":"[Foundation] tsconfig.json with strict TypeScript settings","description":"Create tsconfig.json with strict TypeScript configuration for type safety and ESM module support.

## Context
TypeScript configuration is critical for ensuring type safety, proper module resolution, and IDE support throughout the CASS Memory System codebase. The configuration must support ESM, strict type checking, and clean import paths.

## Technical Specification

### Compiler Options:
- target: ESNext - Use latest JavaScript features
- module: ESNext - ES module output
- moduleResolution: bundler - Modern resolution for Bun
- strict: true - Enable all strict type checking options
- esModuleInterop: true - Better compatibility with CommonJS modules
- skipLibCheck: true - Skip type checking of declaration files for faster builds
- resolveJsonModule: true - Allow importing JSON files
- allowSyntheticDefaultImports: true - Allow default imports from modules with no default export
- forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true - Ensure consistent file name casing
- noImplicitAny: true - Disallow implicit any types
- strictNullChecks: true - Strict null checking
- strictFunctionTypes: true - Strict function type checking
- noUnusedLocals: true - Report errors on unused local variables
- noUnusedParameters: true - Report errors on unused parameters
- noImplicitReturns: true - Report error when not all code paths return a value
- noFallthroughCasesInSwitch: true - Report errors for fallthrough cases in switch

### Path Mapping:
- paths: { @/*: [./src/*] } - Clean imports like import { Playbook } from '@/types'

### Include/Exclude:
- include: [src/**/*] - Include all source files
- exclude: [node_modules, dist] - Exclude dependencies and build output

### Output:
- outDir: ./dist - Output directory for compiled files
- declaration: true - Generate .d.ts files for library use
- declarationMap: true - Generate sourcemaps for declarations
- sourceMap: true - Generate sourcemaps for debugging

## Reasoning

### Why Strict Mode:
1. Catch Bugs Early: Strict null checks prevent null/undefined errors at runtime
2. Better Refactoring: Type errors guide you when making changes
3. IDE Support: Better autocomplete and IntelliSense
4. LLM Integration: Type safety is crucial when parsing LLM responses

### Why ESNext Target:
1. Bun Support: Bun natively supports modern JavaScript
2. Smaller Polyfills: No need to transpile to ES5
3. Modern Features: Use async/await, optional chaining, etc.

### Why Bundler Module Resolution:
1. Modern Standard: Aligns with modern bundlers and runtimes
2. Better Imports: Supports package.json exports field
3. Bun Compatibility: Matches Bun's module resolution algorithm

### Why Path Mapping:
1. Clean Imports: @/types instead of ../../../types
2. Refactor-Friendly: Moving files doesn't break imports
3. Readability: Clear namespace separation

## Acceptance Criteria

- tsconfig.json exists in project root
- strict: true is enabled
- target: ESNext is set
- module: ESNext is set
- moduleResolution: bundler is set
- Path mapping configured for @/* -> ./src/*
- include/exclude properly configured
- outDir set to ./dist
- bun run typecheck runs without errors on initial project
- IDE shows proper IntelliSense for imports
- No implicit any warnings in editor

## How This Serves the Universal Agent Memory System

TypeScript configuration is the quality gate for the entire codebase:

1. Type Safety: Prevents runtime errors when parsing LLM responses, file operations, and config management
2. Developer Experience: IntelliSense and autocomplete make development faster and more reliable
3. Maintainability: Strong types serve as living documentation for the codebase
4. Refactoring Confidence: Type errors guide safe refactoring as the system evolves
5. LLM Output Validation: Zod schemas combined with TypeScript types ensure structured outputs match expectations

Without strict TypeScript, the system would be prone to runtime errors, especially when dealing with dynamic LLM responses and user configurations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:44.254164-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:19:20.048682-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:19:15.791505-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yagh","title":"[Critical] User-Friendly Error Messages","description":"$(cat /tmp/fix_yagh.txt)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:16:46.56704-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T00:24:26.114362-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T00:24:24.947379-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ydz","title":"[LLM] generateSearchQueries - Task-based query generation","description":"Function signature:
async function generateSearchQueries(task: string, config: Config): Promise<string[]>

Schema:
{
  queries: z.array(z.string()).max(5).describe('3-5 diverse search queries')
}

Prompt:
'Given this task: {task}

Generate 3-5 diverse search queries to find relevant information in cass:
- Similar problems encountered before
- Related frameworks or tools
- Relevant patterns or best practices
- Error messages or debugging approaches

Make queries specific enough to be useful but broad enough to match variations.'

Implementation:
1. Build prompt with task
2. Call generateObject with:
   - schema: queries array
   - temperature: 0.5
3. Return queries array

Used by: Generator phase to expand task into multiple cass search angles.

Example: Task 'add authentication' → queries: ['JWT implementation', 'session management', 'password hashing', 'OAuth setup']","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:02.92747-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:16.260667-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:02.188879-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-yee2","title":"Update quickstart and docs for new onboard subcommands","description":"## Context

After implementing the onboard subcommand refactor (cass_memory_system-qwm2), documentation needs to be updated to reflect the new command structure.

## Files to Update

### 1. src/commands/quickstart.ts

Update any references to onboard commands:

Before:
```
cm onboard --sample --json
cm onboard --read <path>
```

After:
```
cm onboard sample --json
cm onboard read <path>
```

### 2. AGENTS.md (if applicable)

Check if there are any onboard command references that need updating.

### 3. README.md (if applicable)

Check if there are any onboard command references.

### 4. Inline help text

Ensure the parent `cm onboard` command help text explains the subcommand structure:

```
cm onboard <command>

Agent-native guided onboarding (no API costs)

Commands:
  status      Check onboarding status and progress
  gaps        Show playbook category gap analysis
  sample      Sample diverse sessions for analysis
  read        Read/export a session for analysis
  prompt      Show extraction instructions
  guided      Full guided workflow
  mark-done   Mark a session as processed
  reset       Reset onboarding progress

Use 'cm onboard <command> --help' for more information on a command.
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All documentation uses new subcommand syntax
- [ ] No references to deprecated flag syntax (except in migration notes)
- [ ] `cm onboard --help` shows clear subcommand list

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","title":"[Critical] Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes","description":"# Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes

## The Problem
Current plan requires manual `cm mark helpful/harmful <rule>` commands.
Users won't do this. They'll use the tool, benefit from rules, but never give feedback.
Without feedback, the confidence system is useless.

## The Solution
Infer feedback automatically from observable signals:

### Signal Sources:
1. **Session Success/Failure**: Did the task complete? Errors? Retries?
2. **Time to Completion**: Fast = rules helped. Slow = rules didn't help or were wrong.
3. **Pattern Matching**: Did the session use patterns matching rules we showed?
4. **Explicit Signals**: User said "that worked!" or "that was wrong"

### Implementation:
```typescript
interface SessionOutcome {
  sessionPath: string;
  startTime: Date;
  endTime: Date;
  rulesShown: string[];  // Rule IDs shown in context
  signals: {
    completedSuccessfully: boolean;  // No error exit
    hadRetries: boolean;  // Multiple attempts at same thing
    userSentiment: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | null;
    taskDuration: number;  // Seconds
    errorCount: number;
  };
}

async function inferFeedback(outcome: SessionOutcome): Promise<FeedbackEvent[]> {
  const feedback: FeedbackEvent[] = [];
  
  for (const ruleId of outcome.rulesShown) {
    let implicit: FeedbackStrength | null = null;
    
    // Strong positive: Quick success with no errors
    if (outcome.signals.completedSuccessfully && 
        !outcome.signals.hadRetries &&
        outcome.signals.taskDuration < FAST_THRESHOLD) {
      implicit = "helpful";
    }
    
    // Strong negative: Errors or retries on tasks using this rule
    if (outcome.signals.errorCount > 2 || outcome.signals.hadRetries) {
      implicit = "not_helpful";
    }
    
    // Explicit overrides implicit
    if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "positive") {
      implicit = "very_helpful";
    } else if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "negative") {
      implicit = "harmful";
    }
    
    if (implicit) {
      feedback.push({
        bulletId: ruleId,
        strength: implicit,
        source: "implicit",
        sessionId: outcome.sessionPath,
        timestamp: new Date()
      });
    }
  }
  
  return feedback;
}
```

### Sentiment Detection (Lightweight):
```typescript
const POSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that worked/i, /perfect/i, /thanks/i, /great/i,
  /exactly what I needed/i, /solved it/i
];

const NEGATIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that('s| is) wrong/i, /doesn't work/i, /broke/i,
  /not what I wanted/i, /try again/i, /undo/i
];

function detectSentiment(sessionText: string): "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" {
  const positiveCount = POSITIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  const negativeCount = NEGATIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  
  if (positiveCount > negativeCount) return "positive";
  if (negativeCount > positiveCount) return "negative";
  return "neutral";
}
```

### Feedback Weighting:
```typescript
const FEEDBACK_WEIGHTS = {
  // Explicit feedback (user ran command)
  "explicit_helpful": 2.0,
  "explicit_harmful": -3.0,
  
  // Implicit from session outcome
  "implicit_helpful": 0.5,
  "implicit_not_helpful": -0.3,
  
  // Implicit from sentiment
  "sentiment_positive": 0.3,
  "sentiment_negative": -0.5
};
```

### Post-Session Hook:
```typescript
// Runs automatically after cass detects session end
async function onSessionEnd(sessionPath: string): Promise<void> {
  // 1. Get rules that were shown for this session
  const contextLog = await loadContextLog(sessionPath);
  if (!contextLog?.rulesShown) return;  // Context wasn't used
  
  // 2. Analyze session outcome
  const outcome = await analyzeSession(sessionPath);
  outcome.rulesShown = contextLog.rulesShown;
  
  // 3. Infer and record feedback
  const feedback = await inferFeedback(outcome);
  for (const f of feedback) {
    await recordFeedbackEvent(f);
  }
  
  // 4. Update confidence scores
  await updateConfidenceScores(feedback);
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Manual feedback has ~0% adoption rate
- Without feedback, confidence scores never update
- System can't learn what works vs what doesn't
- Implicit feedback is "good enough" for most cases
- Can always add explicit feedback later for edge cases

## Trade-offs
- False positives/negatives possible (mitigated by low weights)
- Privacy: Analyzing session content (already in cass, so OK)
- Complexity: Need to track which rules were shown","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:16.860446-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:48:17.022665-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:48:09.838663-05:00","comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Problem: Users Won't Provide Manual Feedback
Nobody will consistently run `cass-memory mark b-xyz --helpful` for every useful bullet.

## Solution: Implicit Feedback Signals
1. Session outcome correlation (bullet shown + success = implicit helpful)
2. Audit-based feedback (violations auto-mark harmful)
3. Agent integration (batch marking)
4. Interactive batch marking mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- Track which bullets shown in each context call
- Correlate with session outcomes
- Implement implicit +0.5 helpful for success correlation
- Add --batch-helpful and --interactive flags

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yj1z","title":"[Test Infrastructure] Test fixtures and factory functions","description":"## Test Fixtures and Factory Functions

Create reusable test fixtures and factory functions for deterministic testing WITHOUT mocks.

### Required Factories:
1. `createTestPlaybook(overrides?)` - Returns valid Playbook with controllable defaults
2. `createTestBullet(overrides?)` - Returns valid PlaybookBullet with all fields
3. `createTestDiary(overrides?)` - Returns valid DiaryEntry
4. `createTestConfig(overrides?)` - Returns valid Config with test-safe defaults
5. `createTestFeedbackEvent(type, overrides?)` - Returns helpful/harmful events

### Design Principles:
- NO mocks - use real data structures
- Deterministic - same inputs always produce same outputs
- Composable - factories can be nested/combined
- Type-safe - full TypeScript inference

### Example Usage:
```typescript
const bullet = createTestBullet({
  helpfulCount: 5,
  harmfulCount: 1,
  maturity: 'proven'
});
```

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","title":"[E2E Tests] Full ACE pipeline - session to playbook","description":"## E2E Test: Full ACE Pipeline - Session to Playbook

### Test Scenario
Complete end-to-end test of the Autonomous Curation Engine:
Session ingestion → Reflection → Validation → Playbook update

### Test Steps

#### 1. Setup
- Create temp directory structure
- Initialize cass-memory config
- Create sample session transcript file
- Create initial empty playbook

#### 2. Execute Full Pipeline
- Run `cm context` with session path
- Trigger reflection (automatic or manual)
- Verify diary entry created
- Verify playbook updated

#### 3. Validate Outputs
- Diary entry has valid structure
- Playbook has new bullets
- Bullets have confidence scores
- Timestamps are correct

#### 4. Verify Logging
- Each pipeline stage logs entry/exit
- Timing information captured
- Error paths logged appropriately
- Verbose mode shows full details

### Test Data
- Sample session with 20+ messages
- Mix of code, discussion, errors
- Clear lessons to extract

### Success Criteria
- Pipeline completes without error
- Diary entry matches session content
- Playbook bullets are relevant
- All intermediate files created

### Verbose Logging Requirements
- Log: 'Starting ACE pipeline for session: <path>'
- Log: 'Reflection phase: extracting lessons...'
- Log: 'Validation phase: checking bullet quality...'
- Log: 'Curation phase: updating playbook...'
- Log: 'Pipeline complete. Added N bullets, duration: Xms'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:28.156899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T01:36:53.861555-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T01:36:53.861555-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-yj1z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:24.284276-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-ep5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:24.445611-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-p4mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:24.570764-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-33w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.148468-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-0thy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.273785-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-z8y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.404948-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-70sd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.525025-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -608,22 +608,22 @@ Recommended protocol for AI agents doing onboarding:
 ## Onboarding Protocol
 
 ### Phase 1: Assessment
-1. Run `cm onboard --status --json` to check current progress
-2. Run `cm onboard --gaps --json` to see category distribution
+1. Run `cm onboard status --json` to check current progress
+2. Run `cm onboard gaps --json` to see category distribution
 3. Decide target: ~20 rules across diverse categories for a good initial playbook
 
 ### Phase 2: Session Analysis Loop
 For each session until target reached:
-1. `cm onboard --sample --fill-gaps --json` → get prioritized sessions
-2. `cm onboard --read <path> --template --json` → get session with context
+1. `cm onboard sample --fill-gaps --json` → get prioritized sessions
+2. `cm onboard read <path> --template --json` → get session with context
 3. Analyze session content, identify 2-5 reusable patterns
 4. Format as rules following extraction guidelines
 5. `cm playbook add --file rules.json --session <path>` → add rules
 6. Repeat with next session
 
 ### Phase 3: Verification
-1. `cm onboard --status` → verify progress
-2. `cm onboard --gaps` → check remaining gaps
+1. `cm onboard status` → verify progress
+2. `cm onboard gaps` → check remaining gaps
 3. `cm stats --json` → verify playbook health
